---
title: "All-Time Leaders"
subtitle: "75 years of Formula 1, served from the dbt marts"
page-layout: full
execute:
  warning: false
---

These tables read directly from the `mart_driver_career`, `mart_constructor_career`
and `mart_season_summary` marts — the tested, dimensional output of the
[data pipeline](data-model.qmd), exported to Parquet.

```{python}
#| label: load
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

DATA = Path("../data")

def load(name: str) -> pd.DataFrame | None:
    path = DATA / f"{name}.parquet"
    return pd.read_parquet(path) if path.exists() else None

drivers = load("mart_driver_career")
constructors = load("mart_constructor_career")
seasons = load("mart_season_summary")
HAVE_DATA = drivers is not None
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if not HAVE_DATA:
    print(
        "::: {.callout-note title='Marts not exported yet'}\n"
        "Run the pipeline to populate this page:\n\n"
        "```bash\n"
        "python -m extract.pipeline backfill --seasons 1950:2026\n"
        "cd transform && dbt build\n"
        "python -m extract.pipeline export\n"
        "```\n"
        ":::"
    )
```

## Most race wins

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAVE_DATA:
    cols = ["full_name", "nationality", "championships", "wins", "podiums",
            "poles", "race_entries", "career_points"]
    top_wins = (drivers.sort_values("wins", ascending=False)
                .head(15)[cols]
                .reset_index(drop=True))
    top_wins.index += 1
    display(top_wins)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAVE_DATA:
    import plotly.express as px
    top10 = drivers.sort_values("wins", ascending=False).head(10)
    fig = px.bar(
        top10.sort_values("wins"),
        x="wins", y="full_name", orientation="h",
        text="wins", color="championships",
        color_continuous_scale="Reds",
        labels={"wins": "Race wins", "full_name": "", "championships": "Titles"},
        title="Top 10 drivers by race wins",
    )
    fig.update_layout(template="plotly_dark", height=480,
                      paper_bgcolor="#1a1a2e", plot_bgcolor="#1a1a2e")
    fig
```

## Most championships

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAVE_DATA:
    champs = (drivers[drivers["championships"] > 0]
              .sort_values(["championships", "wins"], ascending=False)
              .head(15)[["full_name", "nationality", "championships", "wins",
                         "first_season", "last_season"]]
              .reset_index(drop=True))
    champs.index += 1
    display(champs)
```

## Constructors — wins & titles

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAVE_DATA:
    cons = (constructors.sort_values("wins", ascending=False)
            .head(15)[["constructor_name", "nationality", "championships",
                       "wins", "poles", "races_entered", "career_points"]]
            .reset_index(drop=True))
    cons.index += 1
    display(cons)
```

## Champions by season

```{python}
#| echo: false
if HAVE_DATA:
    recent = (seasons.sort_values("season_year", ascending=False)
              .head(20)[["season_year", "champion_driver", "champion_constructor",
                         "races", "drivers"]]
              .reset_index(drop=True))
    display(recent)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
The same marts back a Power BI report — see `serve/powerbi/CONNECTION.md` for the
live BigQuery connection and the suggested measures and visuals.
:::
